note: Each category carries TWO prompt sets. `violation` = the policy breach itself (human nudity/explicit, REAL weapons, illegal drugs and paraphernalia). `review` = the look-alike a human should judge (swimwear/lingerie, toy or replica weapons, tobacco/vape). `negatives` are subtracted from BOTH and exist to kill the true false-positive class: mannequins, statues, sculptures and other non-person figures never flag. Tracks are PROMPT SETS, not tag lookups — the 2177-tag vocabulary has no nudity concept at all, only clothing, and a swimsuit is not nudity.

nudity.label: nudity / sexual content
violation: a nude person
a naked human being
explicit sexual content
a topless woman
a person exposing their genitals
pornography
review: a person in swimwear at the beach
negatives: a mannequin in a shop window
a marble statue of a human figure
a portrait of a fully dressed person

weapons.label: weapons
violation: a real firearm
a person holding a real gun
a handgun
a rifle carried by a person
live ammunition and bullets
a knife held as a weapon in a threatening way
review: plastic toy soldiers with toy rifles
a video game controller shaped like a gun
a museum display of antique swords
negatives: a kitchen knife cutting vegetables on a board
a power drill or hand tool

drugs.label: drugs / drug paraphernalia
violation: marijuana buds
a cannabis plant with leaves and buds
a bag of marijuana buds
a jar of cannabis buds
a grinder full of ground marijuana
a person smoking a marijuana joint
a rolled joint and rolling papers
a glass bong for smoking marijuana
a glass bong with a bowl and a stem
a glass pipe for smoking drugs
a crack pipe
a meth pipe with smoke
aluminium foil with burnt drug residue
lines of white powder cocaine on a mirror with a rolled banknote
a pile of white powder drugs on a table
small plastic baggies of white powder
a digital scale weighing white powder in a bag
a brick of packaged cocaine
a pile of colorful ecstasy pills
loose pills scattered on a table next to a plastic baggie
recreational drug pills in a plastic bag
sheets of LSD blotter tabs
a syringe with a spoon and a lighter for heroin
a used needle and drug injection kit
a syringe and a tourniquet on an arm
dried psilocybin magic mushrooms
a bag of dried magic mushrooms
drug paraphernalia laid out on a table
illegal drugs and drug equipment
review: a lit cigarette in an ashtray
a hookah water pipe with shisha
negatives: a photo of an ordinary everyday object
sugar spilled on a kitchen counter
a green houseplant in a pot
a birthday cake with lit candles
policy_neighbours: a pharmacy shelf with medicine boxes
a medicine cabinet with vitamins and supplements
a doctor giving a vaccine injection in a clinic
a medical syringe on a sterile tray
a glass of beer or wine

fit.violation: AP 0.726 · recall .944 at 1% FP · fitted on 18 hand-verified drug images vs 5145 negatives. tau=0.0191 → 18/18 recall, 79/5145 (1.54%) flagged.
fit.tier_arbitration: violation requires the drug bank to beat the tobacco bank by TIER_MARGIN: drug-slice violations 18/18 -> 15/18 (the other 3 are people smoking, surfaced at review), COCO violations 79 -> 46 (0.9%), tobacco-keyword photos scored as violations 22/128 -> 10/128, and the vape-exhale case demotes to review.
fit.review: WEAK AND SAID SO: tobacco recall at a 1% FP budget is 0.17 (LVIS smoking labels) / 0.15 (Unsplash smoking photos). A cigarette is usually a 20-pixel object and whole-image embeddings do not see it.
fit.caveat: small positive sets (18 violation / 36 review). Wide-CI estimates on ONE corpus, not a benchmark. Recall on cocaine/heroin/meth imagery specifically is UNMEASURED — no labelled image of it exists in any corpus we may use.

note_drugs: drugs: `policy_neighbours` are scored for EXPLANATION ONLY and must never be subtracted — measured, subtracting them collapses AP 0.58 to 0.04 (a clinical syringe is visually identical to a drug syringe). See research/track-drugs.md.
note_tiers: Tier vocabulary is DATA: a category's prompt-set keys are its tiers, ordered alert > violation > review (moderation) and `match` (content, never counted as moderation). Adding a track = adding a spec entry; the reader needs no change. Owning lanes add their own categories.
